name: cdx_ig_46_payer_atm_jun26
description: Bloomberg CDSO CDX.NA.IG.46 100MM USD payer option struck at the ATM forward spread.

provenance.source_detail: Bloomberg CDSO/CDSW/CDSD CDX.NA.IG.46 V1, 100MM USD notional, S531 USD ISDA Swap Curve - Mid, CBBT Mid CDX curve, valuation date 2026-05-07, expiry 2026-06-17, ATM payer struck at 55.2848bp, Spread Vol 36.03%
provenance.regen_command: manual Bloomberg CDSO/CDSW/CDSD screen transcription; see provenance.screenshots

screenshots.description: Bloomberg CDSO Credit Default Swaption screen for CDX.NA.IG.46 V1 100MM USD ATM payer with strike/ATM forward 55.2848bp, spread vol 36.03%, market value 118781.76, IR DV01 228.21, Spread DV01 25352.02, Vega(1%) 3411.78, Theta -1499.93
Bloomberg CDSW underlying CDX.NA.IG.46 terms: 100MM USD notional, 100bp coupon, 53.6264bp traded spread, maturity 2031-06-20, cash amount -2273847, Spread DV01 46963.21, IR DV01 537.99
Bloomberg CDSD forward CDS matrix for CDX.NA.IG.46 using S531 USD ISDA Swap Curve - Mid and CBBT Mid CDX curve, with 5Y spot spread 53.6264bp
Bloomberg benchmark curve rates popup for S531 USD Custom Swap Curve - Mid dated 2026-05-07

meta.swap_curve: S531 USD ISDA Swap Curve - Mid

source_reference.discount_curve_note: Bloomberg S531 USD Custom Swap Curve - Mid benchmark rates are transcribed from the supplied 2026-05-07 benchmark curve popup. The executable MarketContext stores deterministic continuous-rate discount factors derived from those screen rates; assertions remain the Bloomberg CDSO screen values.
source_reference.hazard_curve_note: CDX.NA.IG.46 CBBT Mid curve spot par spreads are transcribed directly from the CDSD forward CDS matrix screen. Hazard knots are properly bootstrapped (sequential Brent root finding, IMM-rolled maturities) from the par_points using `finstack_valuations::calibration::bumps::bump_hazard_spreads` with a zero parallel bump — i.e. the same procedure the cs01/dv01 paths use, applied once at fixture-prep time. The bootstrapped 5Y hazard ≈ 0.0172 (vs the previous spread/(1-recovery) approximation of 0.00894), which roughly doubles the forward protection PV and brings the natural model forward from ~25 bp to ~53 bp — close to Bloomberg ATM Fwd 55.2848 bp without a large per-fixture forward_spread_adjust knob. The remaining ~2 bp residual is absorbed by forward_spread_adjust = 0.000201.
source_reference.vol_surface_note: Bloomberg displays a single Spread Vol of 36.03%; the surface and pricing override are flat at 0.3603.

finstack_reconciliation.expected_outputs_policy: Expected outputs are Bloomberg CDSO screen values. Tolerances are pinned to one Bloomberg-display digit per metric. Residuals are documented below as a remediation backlog, not absorbed into tolerance bands.
finstack_reconciliation.reference_implementation: Bloomberg L.P. Quantitative Analytics, *Pricing Credit Index Options* (DOCS 2055833, March 2012); Bloomberg L.P. Quantitative Analytics, *The Bloomberg CDS Model* (DOCS 2057273, August 2024); CDSO Loss Amount Calculation (DOCS 2151513). The legacy closed-form Black-on-spreads model — used in finstack's old pricer — was decommissioned on the Bloomberg CDSO screen in 2010 (DOCS 2055833 §1.2). finstack now implements the Bloomberg quadrature model `O = P(t_e) · E_0[(V_te + H(K) + D)+]` (DOCS 2055833 Eq. 2.5) with: V_te = ξN(S_te − c)L_te (random swap value at expiry), H(K) = ξN(c − K)A(K) (deterministic strike adjustment), D = ξN_0·loss(t_v) (deterministic loss settlement, currently 0 for this fixture). The lognormal mean m is calibrated by Brent root-finding so that F_0 = E[V_te], where F_0 includes the no-knockout (1−R)(1−q_te) FEP-equivalent for index options only (single-name options knock out on default and skip this term). The legacy Black pricer is retained only under `model_config.cds_option_use_legacy_black_model` for migration testing and is marked `#[deprecated]` in the source.

rpv01_decomposition_diagnostic.interpretation: AOD methodology (ISDA Standard Model analytical vs FinancePy midpoint) contributes 0.001% to RPV01 — not the source of the residual. The 1.77% gap is in the scheduled-coupon sum across the 20 quarterly periods. Tested premium.start=expiry (FinancePy convention with stub period): increased RPV01 to 4.553 (further from Bloomberg). Tested removing BloombergCdswClean final-period +1-day rule: 0.05% impact. Bloomberg's exact convention not publicly documented; remaining residual is structural.

remediation_history: Phase 1 — FEP default-off: Front-End Protection (FEP) for index payer options is now gated on `model_config.cds_option_index_fep_addback` (default false), matching Bloomberg CDSO Market Value display convention. Pedersen 2003 / O'Kane 2008 academic fair-value still recoverable via flag.
Phase 2 — par_spread re-pin: `forward_spread_adjust` recalibrated so the executable par_spread output equals Bloomberg ATM Fwd 55.2848bp exactly.

Phase 3 — underlying_effective_date convention fix: changed from 2026-03-20 (CDSW spot accrual start) to 2026-06-22 (next IMM after expiry, BDA Mon). The prior date dragged a 92-day in-flight first coupon period into the forward CDS RPV01.

Phase 4 — hazard knot re-bootstrap: replaced the spread/(1-recovery) approximation knots with proper sequential Brent bootstrap (IMM-rolled maturities) using `bump_hazard_spreads(curve, ctx, BumpRequest::Parallel(0.0), Some(disc_id))`. Bootstrapped 5Y h ≈ 0.0172 (vs prior 0.00894), roughly doubling the forward protection PV to within 0.5% of Bloomberg's implied value.

Phase 5 — Black T convention fix: rewrote `CDSOption::black_time_to_expiry` to match the FinancePy reference implementation (`CDSIndexOption.value_adjusted_black` in github.com/domokane/FinancePy/financepy/products/credit/cds_index_option.py): `T = (expiry - value_dt) / 365.0` — pure calendar days from the valuation date to option expiry, divided by 365. Replaces the prior `year_fraction(cash_settlement_date, exercise_settlement_date, day_count=Act/360) [+ 1 day inclusive rule]` machinery, which conflated the CDS premium-leg accrual convention with option-pricing time-to-expiry. The day-count rule that governs CDS premium accrual (typically Act/360 for USD) does not apply to the option's vol-scaling clock. Tightens NPV residual from -$5,124 (-4.3%) to -$1,381 (-1.2%) — best-yet alignment to the Bloomberg CDSO screen value.
investigated_but_not_changed: BloombergCdswClean final-period +1-day inclusive rule (`coupon_accrual` in cds/pricer/metrics.rs:189-200): kept. Documented Bloomberg CDSW convention for spot CDS premium-leg accrual at maturity. Removing would change spot-CDS pricing across the codebase. Empirical impact on cdx_ig_46 NPV ~$50.

AOD half-day bias (`cds_aod_half_day_bias` flag, default false): tested by setting true on the synthetic CDS. Empirically moved cdx_ig_46 NPV by ~$14 (-0.01%) — not a meaningful driver. Flag remains user-controllable for QuantLib `IsdaCdsEngine::HalfDayBias` parity.
known_gaps: NPV residual -$1,381 (-1.16%) after Phases 1-5. Reverse-engineered forward RPV01 ≈ 4.51 (finstack with bootstrapped curve, T = 41/365 = 0.11233) vs Bloomberg-implied ≈ 4.46 (assuming Bloomberg uses the same T). The remaining ~1% is a combination of small annuity-construction differences (final-period +1-day rule, schedule micro-conventions) and any T-formula divergence between Bloomberg and FinancePy/textbook (Bloomberg's exact T formula not publicly documented).

Bloomberg displays positive IR DV01 +228.21; finstack registers -32.88 under `CdsOptionDv01Calculator` (rate-bump + hazard rebootstrap from par_spreads). Magnitude residual ~$261. Hypothesis: Bloomberg uses `option_dv01 ≈ underlying_cds_ir_dv01 × option_delta = 537.99 × 0.507 ≈ 273`; needs screen reference to confirm.
Bloomberg Spread DV01 25,352 vs finstack CS01 27,131 (+7.0%, $1,779 over). Parallel-par-spread bump vs Bloomberg's likely partial/scaled convention.

Vega residual -$81 (-2.4%). Theta residual +$40 (-2.7%). Same Black-T/annuity micro-convention family as NPV; vega and theta scale with √T and 1/T respectively, so residuals from any T mismatch propagate to these Greeks.

risk_metric_notes.npv: Bloomberg Market Value is the option premium in USD for 100MM notional.
risk_metric_notes.par_spread: Bloomberg ATM Fwd (bp) is the forward CDS spread used as the ATM strike.
risk_metric_notes.vega: Bloomberg Vega(1%) is the dollar PV change for a +1 absolute percentage point move in lognormal spread vol.
risk_metric_notes.dv01: Bloomberg IR DV01 is the +1bp discount-curve sensitivity displayed on CDSO.
risk_metric_notes.cs01: Bloomberg Spread DV01 is the spread sensitivity displayed on CDSO.
risk_metric_notes.theta: Bloomberg Theta is the displayed one-day time decay in dollars.

npv.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg displays Market Value in USD to cents. A one-dollar absolute tolerance on 100MM notional is a strict source-parity guard; model/source convention drift belongs in inputs.source_reference.finstack_reconciliation, not in tolerance.

par_spread.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg displays ATM Fwd to 4 decimals in bp. Tolerance is one displayed last digit; expected_outputs remains the screen value.

vega.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg displays Vega(1%) to cents. Tolerance is five cents to cover display rounding only.

dv01.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg displays IR DV01 to cents. Tolerance is five cents to cover display rounding only.

cs01.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg displays Spread DV01 to cents. Tolerance is five cents to cover display rounding only.

theta.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg displays Theta per day to cents. Tolerance is five cents to cover display rounding only.
